title: Nondiscreteness of $F$-thresholds
abstract: We give examples of two dimensional normal ${\mathbb Q}$-Gorenstein graded domains, where the set of $F$-thresholds of the maximal ideal is not discrete, thus answering a question by Mustaţă-Takagi-Watanabe.   We also prove that, for a two dimensional standard graded domain $(R, {\bf m})$ over a field of characteristic $0$, with graded ideal $I$, if $({\bf m}_p, I_p)$ is a reduction mod $p$ of $({\bf m}, I)$ then   $c^{I_p}({\bf m}_p) \neq c^I_{\infty}({\bf m})$ implies $c^{I_p}({\bf m}_p)$ has $p$ in the denominator.
